claims.claimText: Meniscus Tear pathoanatomy centers on medial-meniscus, lateral-meniscus, acl and related stabilizers.

claims.claimText: Do not anchor on a single exam maneuver for meniscus tear — integrate mechanism, instability symptoms, and imaging pattern.

claims.claimText: Meniscus tears range from rehab problems to repairable mechanical lesions depending on pattern and symptoms.

claims.claimText: Rehabilitation milestones and return-to-play criteria for meniscus tear require attending-defined progression gates.

claims.claimText: Peripheral longitudinal tears in vascular zones are the classic repairable meniscus lesion.

decisionPoints.trigger: Locked knee with displaced bucket-handle meniscus tear
decisionPoints.action: Urgent arthroscopic reduction and repair or meniscectomy as pattern dictates

decisionPoints.trigger: Active athlete with functional instability or mechanical symptoms from meniscus tear despite appropriate initial management
decisionPoints.action: Discuss operative reconstruction versus continued rehabilitation; graft selection and fixation are attending decisions

decisionPoints.trigger: Athlete approaching return-to-sport after meniscus tear treatment
decisionPoints.action: Apply sport-specific return-to-play criteria with strength, stability, and functional testing milestones
